review_title: Simple, powerful task management that actually works 🦊
description: Any.do made organizing my work and daily tasks much easier. I have been using it for almost 6 years now and it has become part of my daily routine. It is very easy to use, clean and fast. I use it every day to manage tasks, track projects and plan routines. The widget is amazing because it keeps everything visible so I do not forget things. It offers great value for money and works smoothly across devices. Overall it is one of the most practical productivity tools I have used.
author: Mike K.
pros: Excellent task management and very easy to use. Great for project tracking and organizing work. Templates are useful for repeating workflows and routines. Collaboration works well when sharing tasks with others. Sync between devices is smooth and reliable, and the overall value for money is very good.
cons: Customer support is generally good, but sometimes responses can take a bit of time. More customization options for templates or widgets could make the experience even better, but overall it is still a very solid tool.
author_profession: Head of Offensive Cyber Operations , COO

review_title: Any.do never misses a task.
description: One word. Declutter. You'll never work with such ease with Any.do. From my personal life to professional, from picking up meds on my way or keeping my refrigerator stacked with groceries. ANy.do has it all sorted.
author: Sijo A.
pros: I love how Any.do helps me in segregating my tasks as I handle multiple projects and with different timelines. It also helps me in covering my personal to-do's and the it's so easy to use. I really love how the premium version helps to customize to my requirements and the premium price doesn't pinch my pocket at all.
cons: It totally declutters my day. I go about my day with a mental clarity on what tasks I have at hand and how the location based reminders are really a life saver.

review_title: Simple, Handy App That Keeps Me On Track
description: I’ve played around with loads of productivity apps over the years and I’m really enjoying using Any.do. It’s simple, handy, and helps me keep track of my work and day-to-day bits so nothing gets missed. The WhatsApp integration is fantastic because reminders come straight through where I already check messages, so I don't miss appointments. For the price it’s great value and just makes staying organised a lot easyer.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: I’ve been using Any.do and I’ve found it really handy for keeping on top of my work and day-to-day bits. It’s dead easy to use and helps me keep track of what needs doing so nothing slips through the cracks. The WhatsApp reminders are proper useful because they pop up where I already check messages, so I dont miss appointments. For the price it’s great value and just makes staying organised a lot easyer. 👍
cons: I do think it could do with a bit more functionality. Being able to edit notes more properly, like making text bold or using different colours inside the information, would make it easier to highlight important stuff and organise notes better. 👍 I haven't needed to use any customer support yet, but I will request help if I need it.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Hyped. Not Useful in a sense promised
description: Bad. There should be money refund policy available for subscribers. All key features showcased are in initial phases and not developed yet to deliver value to paid subscribers
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Integration with Reminders & Notes. I have bought paid subscriptions, and not at all satisfied with unlocked tools.
cons: All features in any.do - paid version are already available on other native apps of google/apple calendar. Integration of multiple Calendars is not a unique feature. Over & above, the whatsapp integration is hyped. It requires additional payment to zappier, as the basic zaps available on zap are good for no use for sending reminders.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: A clean, reliable task manager that respects your time
description: My experience with Any.do has been consistently positive. It’s one of the few task managers I’ve used that stays out of the way while still being powerful enough for daily professional use. Setup is straightforward, the learning curve is minimal, and it’s easy to build trust in the system quickly. Any.do works particularly well for individuals and small teams who want a dependable, well-designed task manager that encourages regular use rather than constant tweaking. It’s become a tool I actually enjoy opening each day.
author: Pariah B.
pros: Any.do strikes an excellent balance between power and simplicity. The interface is clean and fast, tasks are quick to enter, and recurring items are handled elegantly without getting in the way. I especially appreciate how easy it is to move between daily planning and longer-term task organization without friction. Cross-platform syncing has been consistently reliable for me, and the mobile app feels just as intentional and polished as the desktop experience. Subtasks, reminders, and due-date handling are all thoughtfully implemented and actually support getting work done rather than just managing lists.
cons: Some advanced customization options (views, automation depth, or reporting) are more limited compared to heavier task-management platforms. That said, this feels like a conscious design decision rather than a shortcoming—Any.do prioritizes usability and speed over complexity. For users who want extremely granular project management or analytics, a more specialized tool may be a better fit.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: Positive - Technical Review
description: Any.do is a reliable, efficient task execution tool. It doesn’t try to be everything—and that’s why it works. If your goal/ my goals are to stay organized and actually complete tasks, then Any.do it your one stop shop
author: Luke D.
pros: Any.do is a well-structured task management platform that balances simplicity with practical functionality. It’s designed for users who want fast task capture, clean organization, and reliable cross-device syncing without unnecessary complexity.
cons: If there was one, but only to be picky since there are other variations of these stacks achieving the same goal. It would be the Limited advanced workflow automation compared to tools like Notion or ClickUp

review_title: Minimalism boosting productivity!
description: In an overloaded ecosystem of excessive information, finding a tool that cuts the noise and lets you focus on the essentials without missing important tasks is like finding a needle in a haystack! if you are into minimalism, this is the app for you
author: Erik David G.
pros: The simplicity is priceless! it’s the easiest reminder app to use, I’ve been using it for almost 3 years, and even though I have tried too many other tools, I keep coming back at this one!
cons: So far, there’s nothing I dislike about it. It’s the easiest to operate, and you know, I’ve been using it on a basic level; it's helped me tremendously grow in my daily operations!

review_title: Does everything I need it to. Easy to use
description: It's been a really nice app to organize my thoughts and subsequent future actions. I've been using it for about a week now and it's already helped me redirect down-time towards my goals. I like that it has different categories of goals, too. That way I can remember the long term stuff without any sort of implied pressure to achieve it ASAP. Overall enjoyable app. Would recommend.
author: Eric A.
pros: Overall I really like it. I'm not asking it to change my entire life, and it's not designed as if it needs to. But it's a great app to organize all the little things I need to get done when I hate the chance. I like the reminder alarms that remind me to check my to do list at times when I might otherwise sit idly on the couch.
cons: Color coding is in the paid subscription model. Guys, I can't even make the task red or green without paying for it? I'm a grad student and can't afford gas in my car.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Great project management app
author: Kelly M.
pros: The ease at which this app flows is a refreshing change from everything b else available on the play store. I love how it doesn't make me feel overwhelmed at all to get my day to day tasks done.
cons: I haven't found anything i dislike about it yet but will keep you updated if that gap happens to change.

review_title: sleak design, yet expensive if you upgrade
description: Love the planning and simple design. I use it from time to time whenever I have a lot of projects happening at the same time
author: Adel M.
pros: Great app, love the simplicity of the application; I love how it's also cross platform on desktop and on my phone
cons: Premium features are expensive. $5 a month is a stretch for a planning app; i am okay with $2/month as an example

review_title: Game changer for my agency!
description: It was easy to get up and running, I was able to start using it to send my team relevant tasks, comment quickly without having to rely on a second platform for that, and the dashboard helps me have a complete view of everything going on in every project. And as i mentioned previously, the power of being able to share a project view with our clients in their portal, makes it easy for us, since we don't have to manage two task lists for every client like we had to do with other platforms.
author: Angel Rafael R.
pros: Good task management platform for my team and the feature to share it with external users is great for my clients. Overall the best solution for my agency. And the cost is totally worth it compared to other solutions.
cons: The news and tips section seems a bit annoying at times, but is something we can clearly look over, but it would be better if it wasn't too much of a placeholder.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: 10 years running and STILL my favorite to do app.
description: I’ve been on Any.do for about 10 years now. Hands down the best app for this kind of thing. I’ve used it on my own, in collaboration with work and friends and would highly recommend to someone trying to get all their tasks in order.
author: Quinn W.
pros: Having different folders for different aspects of my life has been a HUGE help. I can really compartmentalize my to do list to the specific project at hand.
cons: There’s not much I don’t like, they’ve been really great about improving the app over the last decade so any complaint I’ve had has been pretty quickly fixed.

review_title: Managing my week made easier with Any.do
description: Overall, this app has helped me manage my schedule, to-do list, and keep track of all of the many details of my day. Having the option to use it as a web browser, app, or widget on my phone has helped me to have many places to track and manage my daily tasks. With my current busy schedule having an easy place to drop a to-do list has been a major game changer and a daily life saver.
author: Penny L.
pros: It is an easy way to keep all of my tasks in one location. I have it as a widget on my phone which helps me to quickly set a reminder of a task I need to complete or an Item I need to add to my schedule. Additionally, having it on both my phone and computer helps me to add to-do's quickly regardless of which device I am working on.
cons: I honestly do not have any major complaints. I have now been using Any.do for the past 3 years and it has majorly helped me be able to track items and keep things from slipping under the rug.

review_title: Easy to learn high value app
description: It's been amazing.So far i'm really impressed with what the team has been able to create, and hopefully we see more apps like this in the future
author: Lincoln G.
pros: I'm very impressed by the features that they have on here. The ability to move the tasks, and also the ability to sync up easily is really great. There's virtually no time to learn.It's very impressive how quickly you can learn how to use this app. The customer support seems like it will be on point and based on the amount of money i'm going to save with this app.It's definitely going to help me schedule a lot of better details.And you know, the templates are pretty amazing, too. The reminders are fast to set up
cons: I haven't had any issues with the application.Actually, it's surprising how fluid it works.It seems like it's been well optimized and will work for a long time

review_title: Amazing To Do app and calendar
description: Excellent experience, in fact, we are sticking with Any.do for long time. Already using it for more than a year.
author: Supriya K.
pros: We are using Any.do for some time now, after trying most of app such as Notion, Todoist and even Microsoft To Do. Any.do is a fantastic app for staying organized and managing daily tasks. The clean interface, reminders, and easy sync across devices make it really convenient. We love how it helps us plan our day and keep track of everything in one place. We liked the calendar feature which syncs Google calendar. Highly recommended for anyone who wants to boost productivity!
cons: One notable drawback of Any.do is the lack of a comprehensive dashboard view on the mobile app, where we cannot see both our main lists and nested subtasks at a glance without drilling down into each one individually.

review_title: Good enough, and very possible to enhance all the cons away.
description: I am using Any.do because my spouse uses it. We needed to have shared tasks app. It doesn't do everything that I would like. I was coming from managing personal tasks with Jira, Google Tasks, and IFTTT. For the simplicity of a shared list I have given up all my customizations.
author: Brian D.
pros: The shared lists feature for families is great. Being able to manage my tasks separately from the shared list is a bonus. I appreciate how it's similar to many more complex project management technologies, but simplier.
cons: It would be great if this integrated with IFTTT and not just Zapier. That's sorta ok, but not everyone wants that product. On the mobile app, tapping back is sometimes confusing and I don't know where am I actually going to end up. Will I go back to the last screen I was at, or will I exit the app completely? Also on the mobile app... it's confusing where am I going to start when I first launch the app. Will it be on myDay, my Lists? Not intuitive to figure this out. Importing tasks from Jira or Google Tasks was hard, and a lot of manual work. You need to setup an automatic archive. We have many recurring tasks that we have to customize everytime to set it as Recurring, and create a new one as soon as the current one is checked. This is a LOT of taps to set every single recurring task. Can you make a "template" that the user can customize?
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Any.do: The essential ally for client management and multi-platform productivity for freelance designers.
description: After a little over two years of daily use, my overall experience has been one of complete satisfaction. Any.do is the key tool that provides me with the professional structure to manage multiple clients and design projects simultaneously. It's a fundamental part of the backbone of my freelance operation, giving me both efficiency and the peace of mind of knowing that my clients are always kept informed of progress. It's a platform I wholeheartedly recommend. Extremely low. Any.do's interface is clean, intuitive, and minimalist, allowing any user to become familiar with creating lists and boards in minutes. Adoption by third parties (clients) is seamless. I used to use other apps like Todoist and MeisterTask, both paid versions... I never went back. Excellent for the professional environment. The permission management gives me great peace of mind. The ability to share a board with a simple public link switch allows me to offer my clients 24/7 tracking of the status of their active projects. This transparency, along with straightforward access management, allows me to work with confidence. A fully justified investment (Premium version). The subscription pays for itself thanks to the productivity features that transform my workflow. One of the most valuable features for me is the WhatsApp integration, which allows me to create tasks instantly via text message and, crucially, receive reminder notifications on my smartwatch without having to pick up my phone. This is a huge time-saver. Its flexibility is ideal for freelancers (though I'd venture to say it's suitable for any type of environment or work). I usually create individual boards for each client, which allows me to customize and organize the stages of each web or graphic design project. Views like "My Day" and "Next 7 Days" are at the heart of my daily planning, ensuring my deliverables are always on time. The scheduling is robust and essential for my business. Recurring tasks are easy to set up and vital for managing website maintenance or scheduling regular client meetings. The most outstanding technical feature is the synchronization: it's seamless and real-time across my iMac (web), iPadOS, and Android. This cross-platform reliability ensures I can check on or schedule a client meeting at any time, without fail. So I can tell anyone interested that I totally recommend Any.do and I'm sure they'll end up loving it as much as I do.
author: Jorge M.
pros: What I love most is the two-way integration with WhatsApp. The ability to receive reminder notifications directly on my smartwatch without interrupting my work on my iMac, even seeing reminders while I exercise... and the ability to create new tasks or boards by sending a simple message, has allowed me to reach an almost instantaneous level of productivity and idea management.
cons: While it's a minor detail, the fluidity of dragging and dropping tasks to reorder them in the mobile version could be improved to match the web version. When handling long lists, the browser version is unmatched in this aspect, but the app...

review_title: A Lifesaver for ADHD Productivity
description: Any.do has genuinely improved my day-to-day productivity. I manage owner multiple businesses, have 3 girls, and I'm a Girl Scout mom. We utilize the family plan to keep us all synced on what's happening. It keeps me and my family organized, reduces mental clutter, and helps me turn chaos into clear, actionable steps. While there’s still room for more flexibility, it’s one of the most valuable tools I use for managing my ADHD and staying on top of work and life.
author: Gwen A.
pros: Any.do has been a game-changer for managing my ADHD. I love being able to brain dump everything at once and then easily categorize my tasks. The app is super intuitive, and the integrations with Google Calendar, ChatGPT, and Zapier take it to another level. For example, I can copy an email, paste it into ChatGPT, and instantly create a task or add it to my calendar. It saves me so much time and keeps me organized across both desktop and mobile. Truly a valuable tool I can’t go without.
cons: I wish there were more customization options for recurring tasks and subtasks. Sometimes the syncing between devices has a slight lag, which can be frustrating if I’m moving quickly.

review_title: The Task Manager I've Always Wanted
description: Overall, excellent experience. Implementing Any.do was a huge asset for our team and allowed us to manage a huge number of projects among busy team members across different time zones and keep moving forward every single day. I'm a huge fan and recommend Any.do anytime I get the chance to do so.
author: Olivia D.
pros: I love the way it integrates with my work flow from my phone. Our team is small and, since we are all moms along side being professionals, Any.do has features that make adding tasks quick and easy with out a lot of extra steps. I love that I can connect with ChatGPT to manage our time and use the speech feature to quickly add and change tasks. Once the app was set up the way we liked it, teaching our team to use it was super fast and within the day we were all using it confidently. I love that its just the features I need without any of the extra steps that come with every other task management system I've used. You can easily change and update tasks, and everything seems to take just one tap. It never takes more time to add a task than I have patience for.
cons: It did take a little experimenting to get all the features working that I wanted, but once I found the right plan and figured out how to get everything connected to ChatGPT and Google, Any.do worked like a charm.

review_title: The best task planner to bug you until you actually do stuff
description: I have always use it, and go premium a few times a year this is the only task planner app that has convince me to pay premium more than once!
author: Leonardo M.
pros: What I really like is the whats app integration that is a game change I think the price is also on point , I would not pay more for an applike this, the app is easy to use you honestly dont need too much support but if you need it their reply is quite fast, scheduling tasks is easy so you wont lose what it is important, lots of templates so you can customize and the dashboard takes you exactly of what you need to see at the start
cons: an integration with telegram and a notes tracker like obsidian would take this app to the next level

review_title: Overall Performance
description: The overall experience of Any.do software is generally positive, with users appreciating its intuitive interface and ease of use. Many users find the "My Day" feature and seamless integration with calendars to be particularly beneficial, allowing for a focused plan to manage daily tasks. The app's cross-device syncing is also well-received, making it convenient to manage tasks anywhere. However, some users note that certain features, such as location-based reminders and advanced recurring tasks, are locked behind a paid premium subscription, which can be limiting for free users. Overall, Any.do is praised for its simplicity and effectiveness in keeping tasks organized without overwhelming users
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: The pros of Any.do software include: User-Friendly Interface: Any.do features a bright and colorful interface that is beginner-friendly, making it easy to navigate and use. Task Management: It allows users to organize tasks, set reminders, and manage schedules in one place, enhancing productivity. Cross-Device Syncing: Any.do syncs across multiple devices, ensuring that users can access their tasks and schedules from anywhere. Integration with Other Tools: It integrates with Google Calendar and Outlook, providing a comprehensive schedule view. Voice Commands: Users can quickly add tasks using voice commands, making it ideal for hands-free productivity. These features make Any.do a solid choice for personal and small team use, although it may lack some advanced project management capabilities compared to other tools
cons: Limited customization: The free version offers limited customization options for task organization. Premium subscription lock: Many powerful features like location-based reminders and advanced recurring tasks are locked behind a paid premium subscription. Basic collaboration features: The basic collaboration features are not as robust as those found in other project management tools. Sync delays: Some users experience occasional delays in syncing tasks across devices. Basic task management: While Any.do is great for organizing daily tasks, it may not be as effective for complex project management needs. These cons highlight the limitations of Any. Do's free version and suggest that users may need to consider upgrading to a premium plan for full access to all features.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: A Smooth and Reliable Task Management
description: My overall experience with Any.do has been very positive. It helps me organize my tasks efficiently and effectively every day, making it easier to stay on top of my priorities. The structure of the app, along with features like categorized lists and MyDay, helped me manage my schedule more consistently and reduced the chances of forgetting important tasks. Overall, it really improved my daily productivity and planning.
author: Delnard C.
pros: What I like most about Any.do is how simple it is to create tasks and keep everything organized. It lets me view all my tasks in a structured way—Today, Tomorrow, Upcoming, and Someday—so it’s easy to track what needs my attention. I also appreciate the ability to create different lists, such as Work, Personal, or even a Grocery List, which keeps everything separated but still easy to access. Another feature I really enjoy is MyDay. When it’s scheduled in the morning, it sends a notification on my phone that prompts me to review and plan the tasks I want to focus on for the day. It’s a helpful way to stay on track and maintain a clear daily workflow.
cons: What I liked the least was mainly the limitations for free users. There weren’t any major issues for me, but some features are locked behind the premium plan, and you can only access more options if you upgrade.

review_title: Any.do best productivity app in the market
description: Excellent. love it! Any.do helps me stay organized without feeling overwhelmed. The clean design, daily planner, and reliable reminders fit naturally into my routine and keep me focused. It’s simple, intuitive, and genuinely makes managing my day easier.
author: Shadrack K.
pros: I'm an entrepreneur based in Nairobi, Kenya. For many years, i sought a quality app to help me plan my day and daily business activities. Any.do was a lifesaver and i have been using it for the last 2 years. What a quality website. It has an excellent user interface and notifications timely without uneccesary intrusion. Long may it continue!
cons: difficulty integrating with google calender. Needs improvement. the second drawback for me is that some of the most powerful features—like advanced reminders and deeper integrations—are locked behind the premium plan. I also find that the project management side can feel a bit limited when tasks get complex, especially compared simple tools on the playstore.

review_title: With Any.do I Can Do Anything!
description: I have really enjoyed Any.do, both the desktop and app versions. It's better than simply receiving a message or an email that can get lost over time. My tasks stay on my board until I remove them and they are always easy to see!
author: Karrie M.
pros: I like that it's a shared platform for assigning tasks. My employer can assign tasks to me, then I can check them off my list, or even comment and tag them to communicate about the specific task.
cons: I would like to be able to get notified for comments on a task that I have assigned. This way if my employer assigns a task, I can comment and they will know. This would help prevent missed communication due to a forgotten tag. Additionally, we have had a few times that we include numbers in our tasks, and it will automatically make that a deadline date.

review_title: Any.do is a Really Great Resource
description: I've had this app on and off for years now and I've been paying for it for a while but I honestly don't think I've ever really utilized it to its full potential. I think that if I was truly to utilize everything Any.do has to offer that I would end up really accomplishing a lot and therefore that's now my plan. I am going to start integrating it a lot more into my day to day life to try to keep me on task.
author: Megan H.
pros: It's very intuitive to add things to your task list and I love the integration that it offers. It's also just super easy to read and there's so many great features that they keep adding to. It helps me keep up with my schedule and now that I saw that it's got a calendar function I know I'm going to utilize that a lot more too.
cons: I think sometimes it's aggravating when it decides to automatically apply reminders or alarms based on what you're typing because sometimes I don't want it to alert but it seems like it's trying to help me out in a way. I appreciate that it's trying to keep me on task but there's sometimes that I'm not actually trying to set up the alert. There's probably a setting that I can adjust but I'm still really getting into it so I'm not positive on that but I have a feeling is something I can change.
